mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer and housing demand channels, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter-ahead horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient real economic activity that offsets higher financing costs, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Direction flips to negative during tightening periods, or predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and the unemployment rate signal a policy-induced economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power with interaction term coefficient insignificant at 5% level, or predicts positive returns during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding two percent confirms sustained economic expansion, prompting immediate positive repricing of equity valuations over the following five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.02, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative 60-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above three percent annually forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation readings above 3% are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is already above 4%.
